title: Ensure Methods with Spring Proxying Annotations Are Public
description: Methods marked with @Async or @Transactional should be public. Spring does not recognize non-public methods, and thus makes no provision for their correct invocation. This can lead to misleading assumptions and potential bugs, especially when private methods are marked with @Transactional, giving a false sense of security.
recommendation: Always make sure that methods marked with @Async or @Transactional are public. This ensures that Spring properly recognizes and invokes them, avoiding potential bugs and misleading assumptions.
impact: Ignoring this rule can lead to incorrect assumptions and potential bugs due to Spring's inability to recognize and correctly invoke non-public methods marked with @Async or @Transactional. Additionally, marking private methods with @Transactional can give a false sense of security.

checks.message: Always make sure that methods marked with @Async or @Transactional are public. This ensures that Spring properly recognizes and invokes them, avoiding potential bugs and misleading assumptions.
